days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring the vibrant city

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: Clearing customs and transferring to the city center via Airport Express. Enjoy the comfortable and efficient ride into the heart of Hong Kong.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong International Airport, LANTAU

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Settle into your luxury accommodations at Hotel ICON, a stylish hotel within walking distance of Tsim Sha Tsui station with stunning harbor views.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Star Ferry Ride
timeline.detail: Experience the iconic Star Ferry across Victoria Harbour. A perfect introduction to Hong Kong's stunning skyline at sunset.
timeline.mapQuery: Star Ferry, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Tim Ho Wan
timeline.detail: Enjoy a Michelin-starred dim sum experience at Tim Ho Wan. Be prepared for a wait, but the baked bun with BBQ pork is worth it.
timeline.mapQuery: Tim Ho Wan, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade Walk
timeline.detail: Stroll along the Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade and enjoy the views of the illuminated Victoria Harbour and the Symphony of Lights show.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Nightcap at Ozone Bar
timeline.detail: Visit Ozone, one of the highest bars in the world located in the Ritz-Carlton, for creative cocktails and breathtaking views of the city.
timeline.mapQuery: Ozone Bar, Hong Kong

localTips: Purchase an Octopus Card for convenient travel on trains and buses.
Arrive early at Tim Ho Wan to avoid the long queues.
Download the Klook app for local discounts on attractions and food.

days.title: Exploring Hong Kong Island
days.subtitle: History, culture, and food

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australian Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a classic Hong Kong breakfast of toast, scrambled eggs, and milk tea at this popular local eatery.
timeline.mapQuery: Australian Dairy Company, Jordan, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Take the Peak Tram
timeline.detail: Ride the historic Peak Tram to the top of Victoria Peak for stunning panoramic views of Hong Kong’s skyline and harbor.
timeline.mapQuery: Peak Tram, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Sky Terrace 428
timeline.detail: Enjoy the breathtaking views from Sky Terrace 428 located at the Peak, an ideal photo opportunity spot.
timeline.mapQuery: Sky Terrace 428, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Taste one of Hong Kong's best wonton noodles. Their handmade noodles are famously delicate and flavorful.
timeline.mapQuery: Mak's Noodle, Central, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit PMQ
timeline.detail: Explore PMQ, a creative hub hosting designers and shops showcasing local art, design, and crafts.
timeline.mapQuery: PMQ, Central, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Ride the Central-Mid-Levels Escalator
timeline.detail: Take a ride on the world’s longest outdoor covered escalator system while exploring the quaint neighborhoods along the way.
timeline.mapQuery: Central-Mid-Levels Escalator, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at The Chairman
timeline.detail: Savor contemporary Cantonese cuisine focusing on fresh ingredients and traditional flavors at this acclaimed restaurant.
timeline.mapQuery: The Chairman, Central, Hong Kong

localTips: Take the MTR for quick travel around the island.
Eat light at lunch to save room for dinner at The Chairman.
Check out local events at PMQ on weekends.

days.title: Cultural Day in Kowloon
days.subtitle: Markets and museums

timeline.title: Breakfast at DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store
timeline.detail: Delight in classic dim sum dishes in a casual setting. This spot is renowned for its variety and flavor.
timeline.mapQuery: DimDimSum Dim Sum Specialty Store, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Wong Tai Sin Temple
timeline.detail: Visit Wong Tai Sin Temple, a famous temple dedicated to the deity Wong Tai Sin. Experience the prayers and rituals of locals.
timeline.mapQuery: Wong Tai Sin Temple, Wong Tai Sin, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit the Kowloon Walled City Park
timeline.detail: Wander through the peaceful Kowloon Walled City Park, a former triad stronghold turned beautiful garden. Learn about its history.
timeline.mapQuery: Kowloon Walled City Park, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Clay Pot Rice
timeline.detail: Experience traditional clay pot rice, which is cooked over an open flame. This dish is a must-try in Hong Kong’s local cuisine.
timeline.mapQuery: Clay Pot Rice, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Shopping at Ladies Market
timeline.detail: Stroll through the Ladies Market for souvenirs, fashion, and local snacks. Practice your bargaining skills with vendors.
timeline.mapQuery: Ladies Market, Mong Kok, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Dive into the rich history of Hong Kong through various exhibits at this educational and engaging museum.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Sister's Kitchen
timeline.detail: Conclude your trip with traditional home-style foods at Sister's Kitchen, known for their authentic flavors and warm service.
timeline.mapQuery: Sister's Kitchen, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

localTips: Bring cash for markets and small eateries.
Check museum timings, as they can be closed on certain days.
Get a local SIM card or Wi-Fi device for easy navigation.

hotels.name: Hotel ICON
hotels.note: Luxurious stay with harbor views, close to Tsim Sha Tsui MTR.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Airport to Tsim Sha Tsui via train
Entry fee for Hong Kong Museum of History

urgent.label: Book Hotel ICON
urgent.note: Best rates can fill up quickly

_warnings.message: Day 1 21:30: "Nightcap at Ozone Bar" — cicchetti/bar keyword at dinner hour (20:00+). Check this is a real dinner restaurant.
